title: Ensure that the --insecure-port argument is set to 0

input_row.title: Ensure that the --insecure-port argument is set to 0
input_row.description: Do not bind to insecure port.
input_row.rationale: Setting up the apiserver to serve on an insecure port would allow unauthenticated and unencrypted access to your master node. This would allow attackers who could access this port, to easily take control of the cluster.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --insecure-port argument is set to 0 .
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the below parameter. --insecure-port=0
input_row.impact: All components that use the API must connect via the secured port, authenticate themselves, and be authorized to use the API. This includes: • kube-controller-manager • kube-proxy • kube-scheduler • kubelets
input_row.default_value: By default, the insecure port is set to 8080.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_insecure_port_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the configuration of the kube-apiserver.
- The specific command to run is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
  ```
- This command retrieves the command-line arguments used to start the kube-apiserver pods.
- The expected output should not contain the `--insecure-port` argument or should have it set to `0`.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The `--insecure-port` argument is set to `0` or not present.
  - **Fail:** The `--insecure-port` argument is set to any value other than `0`.
